items.q: How do you say 'I am eating bread' (some bread) in French?
items.random_wrong: Je mange le pain (which means 'the bread').
items.why: Students drop the partitive. French requires DU/DE LA/DES for 'some' even when English omits it. Skipping it makes the sentence ungrammatical in French.

items.q: How do you say 'I play the piano' vs 'I play football'?
items.correct: Je joue DU piano (instrument) vs je joue AU football (sport).
items.misconception: Same preposition for both - 'je joue piano', 'je joue football'.
items.random_wrong: Je joue le piano, je joue le football.
items.why: Students drop or confuse prepositions. The rule: jouer DE + instrument (de, du, de la); jouer À + sport/game (à, au, aux). Easy way to lose marks in speaking exams about hobbies.

items.q: How do you say 'I am using my phone right now' (emphasising the action is in progress)?
items.correct: Je suis en train d'utiliser mon portable.
items.why: Students translate English '-ing' as a participle. French has no progressive tense - the present 'j'utilise' covers both 'I use' and 'I am using'. For emphasis on the ongoing action, use 'être en train de + infinitive'.

items.q: Which is correct: 'There isn't a swimming pool'?
items.correct: Il n'y a pas de piscine
items.misconception: Il n'y a pas une piscine
items.random_wrong: Il y a pas la piscine
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Il n'y a pas de piscine. “Il n'y a pas une piscine” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which means 'I would like to live in a quieter town'?
items.correct: J'aimerais habiter dans une ville plus tranquille
items.misconception: J'aime habiter dans une ville plus tranquille
items.random_wrong: J'ai habité dans une ville plus tranquille
items.why: It's J'aimerais habiter dans une ville plus tranquille. “J'aime habiter dans une ville plus tranquille” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: How many positive adjectives are in: 'Ma ville est animée, propre et historique mais bruyante et sale'?
items.why: Exact answer: 3. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
